scripts.test:linking: vitest run src/parser/__tests__/directive-extractor.test.ts src/parser/__tests__/graph-builder.test.ts src/linking/__tests__/virtual-file-resolver.test.ts && playwright test -g "resolves relative cross-file navigation and reveals the target node|canonicalizes equivalent link target spellings to the same file in browser navigation|navigates through a real @link click and reveals the fragment target node|keeps the newest async file load when an earlier load resolves later|keeps cross-file hover previews on-screen and dismisses only after leaving node and popup|keeps the hover preview stable while the pointer moves in small steps from node to popup|does not show a stale preview after pointer leave before delayed resolve completes|anchors a delayed hover preview to the node current position after the view zoom changes|anchors a delayed hover preview to the node current position after the viewport moves|invalidates preview cache on reload and uses the target file philosophy|keeps the preview cache bounded and retains recently used entries|shows broken-link state and readable feedback for unresolved targets|shows broken-link state and readable feedback for missing target-node fragments|surfaces malformed @link syntax as a readable author warning in the browser harness|rejects out-of-scope link targets without raw fetch and surfaces the broken state|suppresses cross-file hover previews in stress mode"
scripts.test:render-quality: playwright test -g "keeps shipped example graphs and the stress graph free of node-on-node overlap|keeps representative node and label bounds non-overlapping across zoom levels|keeps shipped example labels readable at minimum zoom without collapsing into overlap piles|clamps extreme zoom requests to the supported min/max range|keeps labels inside rendered non-rectangular shapes|grows rectangular nodes to contain long unbroken labels instead of clipping them|uses current expanded node bounds for hover glow and clears hover on pointer leave|keeps simple-flow edge endpoints on rendered node boundaries|surfaces a readable warning when a non-Blueprint edge passes through an unrelated node|keeps Blueprint wires out of rendered long-label node footprints|renders a visible Blueprint fallback wire and surfaces congestion when routing cannot find a path|routes Blueprint edges deterministically regardless of source edge declaration order|renders self-loops and opposite-direction edges as distinct readable shapes|keeps edge labels clear of nodes and rendered edge paths|fitToView produces finite viewport bounds after reload|re-fits content and keeps the canvas crisp after container resize|fitToView recovers content after the viewport is deliberately stranded off-canvas|reflows controls and keeps the rendered graph usable on a narrow mobile viewport|keeps a stable paint order for node internals and preview popup layers|keeps subgraphs behind edges and edges behind nodes on the main viewport stage|keeps subgraph containers around child content with visible padding|handles degenerate graphs without crashes or NaN render state"
scripts.test:theme: vitest run src/renderer/__tests__/theme-contrast.test.ts src/renderer/__tests__/theme-emphasis.test.ts src/renderer/__tests__/theme-depths.test.ts src/renderer/__tests__/theme-literals.test.ts src/renderer/__tests__/theme-resolution.test.ts && playwright test -g "uses the light narrative palette by default when the system color scheme prefers light|applies distinct subgraph depth fills for every shipped philosophy|keeps hover and selection as distinct visual states that can coexist|emphasizes connected neighbors and edges on hover and selection|keeps hover and selection perceptible in the low-glow breath theme|keeps dimmed context readable and distinct from hidden in light narrative mode|keeps broken-link state visually distinct while the same node is selected and hovered|recolors live node, edge, subgraph, and broken-link states on philosophy switch|keeps the selected top node above an occluding sibling in overlap state|uses the topmost node as the hover target when nodes overlap in screen space"
scripts.test:browser:lifecycle: playwright test -g "supports multiple live renderers on one page|guards lifecycle misuse with clear errors|releases listeners, timers, and canvas ownership state on destroy|recovers after synthetic WebGL context loss|falls back to WebGL when the WebGPU API exists but no adapter is available|pauses and resumes the ticker on visibility changes|stops the ticker after idle and restarts on pointer activity|shows a readable fallback state when renderer initialization fails|shows a readable fallback state when no usable GPU backend is available|recovers from WebGPU device loss when available, or reports adapter constraints without hanging"
scripts.test:browser:relayout: playwright test -g "keeps the stage free of orphaned or duplicate sprites across fold, focus, and philosophy rebuilds|rapid relayout interruptions settle without leaving partial-alpha scene artifacts behind|keeps edge endpoints attached to moving nodes during live relayout animation|keeps live relayout motion free of duplicate or orphaned sprites mid-animation|renders a clean mid-relayout frame without double-drawn node artifacts|moves nodes through a smooth live relayout progression instead of teleporting|preserves fold state when switching philosophy|pauses active relayout motion while hidden and resumes it when visible again|skips relayout fade and motion work when switching layouts in stress mode"
scripts.verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm test:browser && pnpm build && pnpm build:demo && pnpm test:browser:static-demo && pnpm bundle:check && pnpm pack:check
